claim_boundary: Ticket 25 extracts theorem-kernel candidates and shortcut counterkernels from Ticket 24. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Extract a formal kernel from the detector-budget experiment: a proof route that verifies only a fixed finite Li-prefix is refuted by a surrogate off-critical zero whose threshold witness lies beyond the prefix.
attempts.formal_kernel_statement: In the surrogate detector family, finite prefix P is not a uniform RH proof certificate when there exists an off-critical surrogate row with threshold_seen=false up to P.
attempts.obstruction: This is a kernel against a proof strategy, not an RH counterexample. The real target still requires a zeta-zero theorem with a uniform all-height explicit-formula bound.
attempts.candidate_theorem: A valid contrapositive RH proof must provide an explicit N(delta,T) and a zero-interference bound for all off-critical zeros, not a finite prefix check.
attempts.next_experiment: Formalize the surrogate prefix-refutation lemma and then replace the surrogate with zeta-zero bounds.
attempts.claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Promote the Ticket 24 Collatz lift audit into a theorem-kernel candidate. A quotient cycle is not a positive integer cycle unless its valuation word has a positive integral affine fixed point that validates exactly.
attempts.formal_kernel_statement: For an accelerated Collatz valuation word w of length k, total shift s, and affine constant c>0, a positive integer cycle with exact word w can exist only if 2^s-3^k>0, c is divisible by 2^s-3^k, and the resulting odd integer validates the exact valuations. Every listed false quotient cycle fails this kernel.
attempts.obstruction: The lemma eliminates the tested false quotient cycles. It does not prove that all larger quotients have no nontrivial liftable SCC, and it does not prove descent for non-cyclic branches.
attempts.candidate_theorem: Every finite quotient SCC of the accelerated Collatz map either fails the affine lift kernel or is the known 1-cycle; all remaining branches descend under a well-founded exact-cylinder rank.
attempts.next_experiment: Move this affine lift kernel into a minimal formal proof file, then attack the non-cyclic branch rank.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof and no Collatz counterexample found.

attempts.attempt: Extract the finite theorem-kernel from the Goldbach search: the checked range has no counterexample, and the weakest sampled windows identify where an eventual explicit lower-bound theorem must be stress-tested.
attempts.formal_kernel_statement: A finite Goldbach verification kernel can close only N <= B. To prove the conjecture, it must be paired with an independent theorem proving r_2(N)>0 for every even N>B.
attempts.obstruction: The finite kernel is useful but bounded. The missing proof remains an explicit positive lower bound beyond the verified range.
attempts.candidate_theorem: There is an explicit B such that every even N>B has positive Goldbach representation count, and every even N<=B is certified by a reproducible finite verifier.
attempts.next_experiment: Turn sampled weak windows into explicit error-budget constraints for a large-N theorem.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found.

attempts.attempt: Extract the counterkernel against bounded-gap-only proof shortcuts: deleting the right member of every twin pair destroys exact gap 2 while preserving almost all bounded-gap mass excluding gap 2.
attempts.formal_kernel_statement: Any argument whose decisive statistic is invariant under removing exact gap-2 pairs while retaining bounded gaps cannot prove twin-prime infinitude. It must use an exact-gap-2 lower-bound functional.
attempts.obstruction: The counterkernel refutes a proof shortcut, not the twin-prime negation. A proof still needs an unconditional exact gap-2 lower bound at arbitrarily large scales.
attempts.candidate_theorem: An exact-gap-2 sieve weight has a positive lower bound for infinitely many scales and cannot be replaced by a bounded-gap-only statistic.
attempts.next_experiment: Search for an exact-gap weight with explicit parity-error control.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof. The bounded-gap counterkernel only rejects weaker proof routes.
